muscle_gain.name: Chicken Breast
muscle_gain.benefits: Lean, versatile, and high in protein with minimal fat — ideal for building muscle without excess calories.

muscle_gain.name: Eggs
muscle_gain.benefits: Complete protein with all essential amino acids. Egg yolks contain healthy fats and vitamins like B12 and D.

muscle_gain.name: Salmon
muscle_gain.benefits: Rich in omega-3 fatty acids which reduce muscle inflammation and support recovery and joint health.

muscle_gain.name: Greek Yogurt
muscle_gain.benefits: Contains both fast-digesting whey and slow-digesting casein protein, great for muscle growth at any time.

muscle_gain.name: Tuna
muscle_gain.benefits: Very high protein-to-calorie ratio, affordable, and rich in selenium and omega-3s.

muscle_gain.name: Brown Rice
muscle_gain.benefits: Complex carbohydrate that provides sustained energy for intense training sessions. Pairs well with protein.

muscle_gain.name: Sweet Potato
muscle_gain.benefits: Rich in complex carbs, vitamin A, and antioxidants. Replenishes muscle glycogen efficiently.

muscle_gain.name: Beef (Lean)
muscle_gain.benefits: High in creatine, zinc, and iron — essential nutrients that support muscle growth and energy production.

fat_loss.name: Broccoli
fat_loss.benefits: Very low in calories, high in fiber, and loaded with vitamins C and K. Keeps you full without adding many calories.

fat_loss.name: Spinach
fat_loss.benefits: Extremely nutrient-dense with almost no calories. Contains iron, folate, and antioxidants to support metabolism.
fat_loss.best_time: Any meal, especially salads or smoothies

fat_loss.name: Cottage Cheese
fat_loss.benefits: High in casein protein which is slow-digesting, helping you stay full longer and preserve muscle while cutting.
fat_loss.best_time: Before bed or as a snack

fat_loss.name: Avocado
fat_loss.benefits: Healthy monounsaturated fats that improve satiety and support fat-soluble vitamin absorption. Rich in fiber.

fat_loss.name: Green Tea
fat_loss.benefits: Contains catechins and caffeine that mildly boost metabolic rate and may increase fat oxidation.

fat_loss.name: Apple
fat_loss.benefits: High in fiber and water content, making it very filling. Natural sugars provide quick energy without large calorie load.

fat_loss.name: Turkey Breast
fat_loss.benefits: One of the leanest animal proteins available. Very low fat content while providing complete amino acids.

fat_loss.name: Oats
fat_loss.benefits: Complex carbs with high fiber (beta-glucan) that slows digestion and promotes satiety. Low glycemic index.

high_protein_vegetarian.name: Lentils
high_protein_vegetarian.benefits: Excellent plant-based protein and fiber source. High in iron and folate. Very versatile in cooking.

high_protein_vegetarian.name: Chickpeas
high_protein_vegetarian.benefits: Rich in protein and dietary fiber. Contains complex carbs for energy and significant amounts of folate and manganese.
high_protein_vegetarian.best_time: Lunch, dinner or as a snack (roasted)

high_protein_vegetarian.name: Tofu
high_protein_vegetarian.benefits: Complete plant protein containing all 9 essential amino acids. Also provides calcium, iron, and manganese.

high_protein_vegetarian.name: Paneer
high_protein_vegetarian.benefits: Indian cottage cheese extremely popular for vegetarian muscle building. High in protein and casein.

high_protein_vegetarian.name: Edamame
high_protein_vegetarian.benefits: Young soybeans rich in complete protein, fiber, and various vitamins. Great low-calorie protein snack.

high_protein_vegetarian.name: Black Beans
high_protein_vegetarian.benefits: High protein and fiber content supports muscle recovery and gut health. Rich in antioxidants.

high_protein_vegetarian.name: Quinoa
high_protein_vegetarian.benefits: One of the few plant foods that is a complete protein. Also gluten-free and high in magnesium.
high_protein_vegetarian.best_time: Any meal as a rice substitute

high_protein_vegetarian.name: Hemp Seeds
high_protein_vegetarian.benefits: Among the highest plant-based proteins. Rich in omega-3 and omega-6 in an ideal ratio. Easy to add to meals.

budget_friendly.name: Eggs
budget_friendly.benefits: One of the cheapest complete protein sources available. Versatile, easy to cook, and nutrient-dense.

budget_friendly.name: Canned Tuna
budget_friendly.benefits: Extremely affordable, high-protein, and requires no cooking. Long shelf life makes it a staple fitness food.

budget_friendly.name: Peanut Butter
budget_friendly.benefits: Calorie-dense and protein-rich. Healthy fats and protein make it a great addition to shakes or meals for bulking.

budget_friendly.name: Oats
budget_friendly.benefits: Excellent calorie-efficient carbohydrate source with good protein content. Very affordable per serving.

budget_friendly.name: Lentils
budget_friendly.benefits: Dirt cheap source of protein and fiber. Can be bought dry and stored for months, making it incredibly economical.

budget_friendly.name: Milk
budget_friendly.benefits: Contains both whey and casein protein. Rich in calcium and vitamin D. Affordable and effective for muscle gain.

budget_friendly.name: Frozen Vegetables
budget_friendly.benefits: Flash-frozen at peak ripeness, retaining most nutrients. Very affordable and convenient. Essential micronutrients.
budget_friendly.best_time: Any meal as a side dish

budget_friendly.name: Banana
budget_friendly.benefits: Cheap, portable, and quick energy source. High in potassium which helps prevent muscle cramps.
